content: The Canada Business Corporations Act (CBCA) 2024 ESG Amendment introduces mandatory sustainability disclosure requirements for federally incorporated companies. Companies with 20 or more employees operating in Canada are required to publish an annual sustainability disclosure covering material environmental, social, and governance information. Disclosures must include: (1) A description of the company's approach to ESG risk management. (2) Material environmental metrics including greenhouse gas emissions. (3) Social indicators such as workforce diversity, employee health and safety, and community impact. (4) Governance structures for ESG oversight, including board-level accountability. Non-compliance may result in regulatory enforcement action. Companies without a published annual sustainability disclosure are classified as non-compliant. This requirement applies regardless of whether the company is publicly traded.
